name: tessera-playtest-player
description: Single-role child workflow for the orchestrator. One Daytona container, one Playwright context, one player playing one role (gm, builder, guider, or observer) for the duration of the round. Returns per-player findings + latency samples as JSON.

init_params.host_token: (filled in by orchestrator; only used by the GM child to claim the host session)

play.instruction:
You are playing **one role** in a live Tessera workshop. Your assignment:

```json
{{assignment}}
```

Game URL: {{tessera_url}}
Game code: {{code}}
Host token (for GM only): {{host_token}}
Scenario config:
```json
{{scenario}}
```

## Setup
1. `npx --yes playwright install chromium` (the prism-playwright snapshot is missing the binary).
2. Open ONE Playwright browser context. Don't open more — multi-context cookie collisions are a known footgun.
3. Note the wall-clock time you start (`joined_at`) for the report.

## Per-role playbook

### If role == "gm"
1. Claim the host session: navigate to `{{tessera_url}}/host-recover/{{code}}`, paste the host_token in the form, submit. You should be redirected to `/g/{{code}}/master`.
   - If the page-level form is finicky, you can also do this via direct API call: `fetch('/api/games/{{code}}/host-recover', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ token: '{{host_token}}' }) })` from a navigated tessera page so cookies attach. Then refresh `/g/{{code}}/master`.
2. Wait until **9 players** appear in the lobby. Watch the dashboard's lobby panel — it counts unallocated members.
3. Click the numeric `pairs` input in the auto-allocate block, set it to `3`, click "Create 3 pairs".
4. Click "Auto-assign 3 observers" once it's enabled (it's gated on having pairs + lobby members).
5. Set complexity from `scenario` (use the +/− stepper next to the start button) and duration (the free-text field next to it; e.g. enter `10` for 10 minutes if `scenario.round_duration_sec` is 600). Click Start round 1.
6. While the round runs:
   - Switch focused pair every ~90s by clicking pairs in the PairsPanel.
   - Fire any `scenario.trigger_super_powers[]` at the configured `at_seconds` offsets. For `scope: "pair"`, focus that pair_idx first; for `scope: "all"`, switch to All pairs in the AccelerantsRail toggle.
   - For each super-power fired, capture (a) GM dashboard state before/after, (b) wall-clock latency from click to broadcast.
7. When the timer hits 0, observe round-end view. End the game.
8. Report findings on: GM dashboard responsiveness with 3 active pairs, pair-switching delay, super-power broadcast latency, allocation UX with 9 lobby members.

### If role == "builder"
1. Navigate to `{{tessera_url}}/g/{{code}}/join`. Enter `{{assignment.name}}`. Submit.
2. You'll land in lobby. Wait for the GM to assign you a pair + start the round.
3. After round start:
   - Open your sealed brief envelope (tap to open). The gate should drop.
   - Place pieces. Aim for ~10–14 placements over the round. Use a mix of shapes from the tray + colors from the palette.
   - Sample latency at 5 placements: time from click to piece visible (`place_to_visible`).
   - Tap **Test solution** at least 2x — once early (≥3 placements) and once late (≥10 placements). Capture the celebration animation + score change.
   - Try **Clear all** ONCE: tap, observe "Tap again to clear" arming, tap again, confirm wipe.
4. When the round ends, observe the RoundEndedView; read the debrief prompts.
5. Report findings on: place-piece responsiveness (any visible stutter? optimistic→server gap?), test-solution feedback clarity, brief envelope dismissal flow, pair-name nudge UX (if it appeared), Clear-all confirm safety.

### If role == "guider"
1. Navigate to `{{tessera_url}}/g/{{code}}/join`. Name = `{{assignment.name}}`.
2. Wait for pair assignment + round start.
3. After start: open your brief, observe the goal pattern. Read it. Sample readability at the configured complexity.
4. Within the first 60s, click the PairNameBadge (yellow when unnamed) and rename the pair to "The Pelicans". Capture the wall-clock delay until you see the name reflected in the GM tab (you can't directly observe the GM tab, so log the rename click time and trust the orchestrator to correlate with the GM's report).
5. If the orchestrator's scenario fires `agile_share` for your pair, watch the builder snapshot thumbnail appear. Click it; verify full-screen modal opens; Esc to close.
6. Try minimizing the brief (− button) and re-expanding (click the seal circle).
7. Report findings on: goal canvas readability at complexity {{scenario.complexity}}, brief minimize/seal flow, pair-rename UX, super-power notification clarity, brief overlay vs canvas (should NOT overlay anymore — flag if it does).

### If role == "observer"
1. Navigate to `{{tessera_url}}/g/{{code}}/join`. Name = `{{assignment.name}}`.
2. Wait for GM to assign you to a pair as observer.
3. After round start: observe the side-by-side builder + goal layout. Sample: time from join → first goal canvas mounted.
4. If `available_pairs.length > 1`, switch to a different pair at least once.
5. Report findings on: observer view scaling at complexity {{scenario.complexity}}, pair-switcher UX, missing affordances (does observer have any agency? should they?), realtime updates lag from builder activity.

## Universal observations

At the end of the round, also note:
- Any console errors (collect from devtools).
- Any network errors / failed requests.
- General UX surprises — copy that confused you, affordances you missed, transitions that felt rough.

## Final output (stdout JSON)

The LAST thing you print MUST be a single JSON object matching this schema:

```json
{
  "role": "<your role>",
  "name": "<your name>",
  "pair_idx": <0-indexed or null>,
  "joined_at": "ISO8601",
  "round_started_at": "ISO8601 or null",
  "session_duration_sec": <int>,
  "interactions": {
    "placements_attempted": <int>,
    "placements_succeeded": <int>,
    "test_solution_taps": <int>,
    "score_final": <int or null>
  },
  "latency_samples_ms": {
    "place_to_visible": [<int>, ...],
    "broadcast_to_ui_update": [<int>, ...]
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "blocker | major | minor | nit",
      "category": "ux-confusion | bug | slowness | copy | accessibility | visual | performance",
      "title": "...",
      "detail": "...",
      "evidence": "screenshot path or console excerpt or null"
    }
  ],
  "console_errors": [],
  "network_errors": []
}
```

Leave fields irrelevant to your role at sensible defaults (gm has no `place_to_visible` samples; observer has no `score_final`). Put scratch / narrative text ABOVE the JSON; the orchestrator parses only the LAST valid JSON object on stdout.
